author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is called, “A Psalm, to the end, for the sons of Korah, for things secret.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Secret is it then; but He Himself, who in the place of Calvary was crucified, ye know, hath rent the veil, that the secrets of the temple might be discovered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore since the Cross of our Lord was a key, whereby things closed might be opened; let us trust that He will be with us, that these secrets may be revealed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is said, “To the end,” always ought to be understood of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But The End He is called, not because He consumeth, but because He perfecteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ended call we the food which is eaten, and ended the coat which is woven, the former to consumption, the latter to perfection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then we have not where to go farther when we have come to Christ, Himself is called the end of our course.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor ought we to think, that when we have come to Him, we ought to strive any further to come also to the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this thought Philip also, when he said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father, and it sufficeth us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he said, “It sufficeth us,” he sought the end of satisfaction and perfection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then said He, “Have I been so long time with you, and hast thou not known Me, Philip: he that hath seen Me, hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him then have we the Father, because He is in the Father, and the Father in Him, and He and His Father are One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our God is a refuge and strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are some refuges wherein is no strength, whereto when any fleeth, he is more weakened than strengthened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou fleest, for example, to some one greater in the world, that thou mayest make thyself a powerful friend; this seemeth to thee a refuge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet so great are this world’s uncertainties, and so frequent grow the ruins of the powerful day by day, that when to such refuge thou art come, thou beginnest to fear more than ever therein.…Our refuge is not such, but our refuge is strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thither we have fled, we shall be firm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A helper in tribulations, which find us out too much.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tribulations are many, and in every tribulation unto God must we flee; whether it be a tribulation in our estate, or in our body’s health, or about the peril of those dearest to us, or any other thing necessary to the sustaining of this life, refuge ought there to be none at all to a Christian man, other than his Saviour, other than his God, to whom when he has fled, he is strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he will not in himself be strong, nor will he to himself be strength, but He will be his strength, who has become his refuge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, dearly beloved, among all tribulations of the human soul is no greater tribulation than the consciousness of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there be no wound herein, and that be sound within man which is called conscience, wherever else he may suffer tribulation, thither will he flee, and there find God.…Ye see, dearly beloved, when trees are cut down and proved by the carpenters, sometimes in the surface they seem as though injured and rotten; but the carpenter looks into the inner marrow as it were of the tree, and if within he find the wood sound, he promises that it will last in a building; nor will he be very anxious about the injured surface, when that which is within he declares sound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, to man anything more inward than conscience is not found; what then profits it, if what is without is sound, and the marrow of conscience has become rotton?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are close and vehement overmuch, and as this Psalm saith, too great tribulations; yet even in these the Lord hath become a helper by forgiving sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the consciences of the ungodly hateth nothing save indulgence; for if one saith he hath great tribulations, being a confessed debtor to the treasury, when he beholdeth the narrowness of his estate, and seeth that he cannot be solvent; if on account of the distrainers every year hanging over him, he saith that he suffereth great tribulations, and doth not breathe freely except in hope of indulgence, and that in things earthly; how much more the debtor of penalties out of the abundance of sins: when shall he pay what he owes out of his evil conscience, when if he pay, he perisheth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to pay this debt, is to undergo the penalties.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remaineth then that of His indulgence, we may be secure, yet so that, indulgence received, we return not again to contract debts.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then, such security received, what say they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore will not we fear, when the earth shall be confounded” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just before anxious, suddenly secure; out of too great tribulations set in great tranquillity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in them Christ was sleeping, therefore were they tossed: Christ awoke (as but now we heard out of the Gospel), He commanded the winds, and they were still.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since Christ is in each man’s heart by faith, it is signified to us, that his heart as a ship in this world’s tempest is tossed, who forgetteth his faith: as though Christ sleeping it is tossed, but Christ awaking cometh tranquillity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, the Lord Himself, what said He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Where is your faith?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ aroused, aroused up faith, that what had been done in the ship, might be done in their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A helper in tribulations, which found us out too much.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He caused that therein should be great tranquillity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what tranquillity: “Therefore will not we fear when the earth shall be confounded, and the mountains shall be carried into the heart of the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then we shall find not fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us seek mountains carried, and if we can find, it is manifest that this is our security.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord truly said to His disciples, “If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say to this mountain, Be Thou removed, and be Thou cast into the sea, and it shall be done.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Haply “to this mountain,” He said of Himself; for He is called a Mountain: “It shall come to pass in the last days, that the mountain of the Lord shall be manifest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this Mountain is placed above other mountains; because the Apostles also are mountains, supporting this Mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore followeth, “In the last days the Mountain of the Lord shall be manifest, established in the top of the mountains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore passeth It the tops of all mountains, and on the top of all mountains is It placed; because the mountains are preaching The Mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the sea signifieth this world, in comparison of which sea, like earth seemed the nation of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was not covered over with the bitterness of idolatry, but, like dry land, was surrounded with the bitterness of the Gentiles as with sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was to be, that the earth be confounded, that is, that nation of the Jews; and that the mountains be carried into the heart of the sea, that is, first that great Mountain established in the top of the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He deserted the nation of the Jews, and came among the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was carried from the earth into the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who carrying Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostles, to whom He had said, “If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say to this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea, and it shall be done:” that is, through your most faithful preaching it shall come to pass, that this mountain, that is, I Myself, be preached among the Gentiles, be glorified among the Gentiles, be acknowledged among the Gentiles, and that be fulfilled which was predicted of Me, “A people whom I have not known shall serve Me.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The waters thereof roared, and were troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): when the Gospel was preached, “What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seemeth to be a setter forth of strange gods:” this the Athenians; but the Ephesians, with what tumult would they have slain the Apostles, when in the theatre, for their goddess Diana, they made such an uproar, as to be shouting, “Great is Diana of the Ephesians!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amidst which waves and roaring of the sea, feared not they who to that refuge had fled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, the Apostle Paul would enter in to the theatre, and was kept back by the disciples, because it was necessary that he should still abide in the flesh for their sakes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet, “the waters thereof roared, and were troubled: the mountains shook at the mightiness thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whose might?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sea’s? or rather God’s, of whom was said, “refuge and strength, a helper in tribulations, which have found us out too much?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For shaken were the mountains, that is, the powers of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For one thing are the mountains of God, another the mountains of the world: the mountains of the world, they whose head is the devil, the mountains of God, they whose Head is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by these mountains were shaken those mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then gave they their voices against Christians, when the mountains were shaken, the waters roaring; for the mountains were shaken, and there was made a great earthquake, with quaking of the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But against whom this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against the City founded upon a rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The waters roar, the mountains shake, the Gospel being preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, the City of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The streams of the river make glad the City of God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the mountains shake, when the sea rages, God deserteth not His City, by the streams of the river.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these streams of the river?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That overflowing of the Holy Spirit, of which the Lord said, “If any man thirst, let him come unto Me, and drink.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that believeth on Me, out of his bosom shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These rivers then flowed out of the bosom of Paul, Peter, John, the other Apostles, the other faithful Evangelists.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since these rivers flowed from one river, many “streams of the river make glad the City of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that ye might know this to be said of the Holy Spirit, in the same Gospel next said the Evangelist, “But this spake He of the Spirit, which they that were to believe on Him should receive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Holy Ghost was not yet given, because that Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jesus being glorified after His Resurrection, glorified after His Ascension, on the day of Pentecost came the Holy Spirit, and filled the believers, who spake with tongues, and began to preach the Gospel to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence was the City of God made glad, while the sea was troubled by the roaring of its waters, while the mountains were confounded, asking what they should do, how drive out the new doctrine, how root out the race of Christians from the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against the streams of the river making glad the City of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thereby showed He of what river He spake; that He signified the Holy Spirit, by “the streams of the river make glad the City of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Most High hath sanctified His tabernacle:” since then there followeth the mention of Sanctification, it is manifest that these streams of the river are to be understood of the Holy Spirit, by whom is sanctified every godly soul believing in Christ, that it may be made a citizen of the City of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God is in the midst of her: she shall not be moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the sea rage, the mountains shake; “God is in the midst of her: she shall not be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in the midst of her”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That God stands in any one place, and they surround Him who believe in Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then is God circumscribed by place; and broad that which surroundeth, narrow that which is surrounded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No such thing imagine of God, who is contained in no place, whose seat is the conscience of the godly: and so is God’s seat in the hearts of men, that if man fall from God, God in Himself abideth, not falleth like one not finding where to be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For rather doth He lift up thee, that thou mayest be in Him, than so lean upon thee, as if thou withdraw thyself, to fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself if He withdraw, fall wilt thou: thyself if thou withdraw, fall will not He.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is, “God is in the midst of her”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It signifieth that God is equal to all, and accepteth not persons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as that which is in the middle has equal distances to all the boundaries, so God is said to be in the middle, because He consulteth equally for all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God is in the midst of her: she shall not be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore shall she not be moved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because God is in the midst of her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is “the Helper in tribulations that have found us out too much.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God shall help her with His Countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “with His Countenance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With manifestation of Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How manifests God Himself, so as that we see His Countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have already told you; ye have learned God’s Presence; we have learned it through His works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When from Him we receive any help so that we cannot at all doubt that it was granted to us by the Lord, then God’s Countenance is with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heathen are troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how troubled? why troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To cast down the City of God, in the midst whereof is God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To overthrow the tabernacle sanctified, which God helpeth with His Countenance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: with a wholesome trouble are the heathen now troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the kingdoms are bowed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bowed, saith He, are the kingdoms; not now erected that they may rage, but bowed that they may adore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When were the kingdoms bowed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When that came to pass which was predicted in another Psalm, “All kings shall fall down before Him, all nations shall serve Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What cause made the kingdoms to bow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Most High gave His Voice, and the earth was moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fanatics of idolatry, like frogs in the marshes, clamoured, the more tumultuously, the more sordidly, in filth and mire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is the brawling of frogs to the thunder of the clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For out of them “the Most High gave His Voice, and the earth was moved:” He thundered out of His clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what are His clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Apostles, His preachers, by whom He thundered in precepts, lightened in miracles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same are clouds who are also mountains: mountains for their height and firmness, clouds for their rain and fruitfulness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these clouds watered the earth, of which it was said, “The Most High gave His Voice, and the earth was moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is of those clouds that He threateneth a certain barren vineyard, whence the mountains were carried into the heart of the sea; “I will command,” saith He, “the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was fulfilled in that which I have mentioned, when the mountains were carried into the heart of the sea; when it was said, “It was necessary that the word of God should have been spoken first to you; but seeing ye put it from you, we turn to the Gentiles;” then was fulfilled, “I will command the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The nation of the Jews hath just so remained as a fleece dry upon the ground.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this, ye know, happened in a certain miracle, the ground was dry, the fleece only was wet, yet rain in the fleece appeared not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also the mystery of the New Testament appeared not in the nation of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What there was the fleece, is here the veil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the fleece was veiled the mystery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But on the ground, in all the nations open lieth Christ’s Gospel; the rain is manifest, the Grace of Christ is bare, for it is not covered with a veil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that the rain might come out of it, the fleece was pressed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by pressure they from themselves excluded Christ, and the Lord now from His clouds raineth on the ground, the fleece hath remained dry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of them then “the Most High gave His Voice,” out of those clouds; by which Voice the kingdoms were bowed and worshipped.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord of Hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our taker up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any man, not any power, not, in short, Angel, or any creature either earthly or heavenly, but “the Lord of Hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our taker up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who sent Angels, came after Angels, came that Angels might serve Him, came that men He might make equal to Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mighty Grace!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If God be for us, who can be against us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord of Hosts is with us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Lord of Hosts is with us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If” (I say) “God be for us, who can be against us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that spared not His own Son, but delivered Him up for us all; how hath He not with Him also freely given us all things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore be we secure, in tranquillity of heart nourish we a good conscience with the Bread of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord of Hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our taker up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: However great be thy infirmity, see who taketh thee up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One is sick, a physician is called to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His own taken-up, the Physician calleth the sick man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath taken him up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great hope of salvation; a great Physician hath taken him up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Physician?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every Physician save He is man: every Physician who cometh to a sick man, another day can be made sick, beside Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The God of Jacob is our taker up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make thyself altogether as a little child, such as are taken up by their parents.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those not taken up, are exposed; those taken up are nursed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thinkest thou God hath so taken thee up, as when an infant thy mother took thee up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so, but to eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thy voice is in that Psalm, “My father and my mother forsake me, but the Lord hath taken me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come and see the works of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now of this taking up, what hath the Lord done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider the whole world, come and see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou comest not, thou seest not; if thou seest not, thou believest not; if thou believest not, thou standest afar off: if thou believest thou comest, if thou believest thou seest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how came we to that mountain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not on foot?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it by ship?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it on the wing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it on horses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all that pertain to space and place, be not concerned, trouble not thyself, He cometh to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For out of a small stone He hath grown, and become a great mountain, so that He hath filled all the face of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then wouldest thou by land come to Him, who filleth all lands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, He hath already come: watch thou.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By growing He waketh even sleepers; if yet there is not in them so deep sleep, as that they be hardened even against the mountain coming; but they hear, “Awake, thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was a great thing for the Jews to see the stone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the stone was yet small: and small they deservedly despised it, and despising they stumbled, and stumbling they were broken; remains that they be ground to powder.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so was it said of the stone, “Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is one thing to be broken, another to be ground to powder.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be broken is less than to be ground to powder: but none grindeth He coming exalted, save whom He brake lying low.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now before His coming He lay low before the Jews, and they stumbled at Him, and were broken; hereafter shall He come in His Judgment, glorious and exalted, great and powerful, not weak to be judged, but strong to judge, and grind to powder those who were broken stumbling at Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “A stone of stumbling and a rock of offence,” is He to them that believe not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, no wonder if the Jews acknowledged not Him, whom as a small stone lying before their feet they despised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are to be wondered at, who even now so great a mountain will not acknowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews at a small stone by not seeing stumbled; the heretics stumble at a mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now that stone hath grown, now say we unto them, Lo, now is fulfilled the prophecy of Daniel, “The stone that was small became a great mountain, and filled the whole earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore stumble ye at Him, and go not rather up to Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is so blind as to stumble at a mountain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Came He to thee that thou shouldest have whereat to stumble, and not have whereto to go up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come ye, and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Isaiah saith this: “Come ye, and let us go up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Come ye, and let us go up”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come ye,” is, Believe ye.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us go up,” is, Let us profit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they will neither come, nor go up, nor believe, nor profit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They bark against the mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now by so often stumbling on Him they are broken, and will not go up, choosing always to stumble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say we to them, “Come ye, and see the works of the Lord:” what “prodigies He hath set forth through the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Prodigies are called, because they portend something, those signs of miracles which were done when the world believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what thereafter came to pass, and what did they portend?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This not yet see we fulfilled: yet are there wars, wars among nations for sovereignty; among sects, among Jews, Pagans, Christians, heretics, are wars, frequent wars, some for the truth, some for falsehood contending.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet then is this fulfilled, “He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth;” but haply it shall be fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or is it now also fulfilled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In some it is fulfilled; in the wheat it is fulfilled, in the tares it is not yet fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this then, “He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wars He calleth whereby it is warred against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who warreth against God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ungodliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what to God can ungodliness do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth an earthen vessel dashed against the rock, however vehemently dashed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With so much greater harm to itself it cometh, with how much the greater force it cometh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These wars were great, frequent were they.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against God fought ungodliness, and earthen vessels were dashed in pieces, even men by presuming on themselves, by too much prevailing by their own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is that, the shield whereof Job also named concerning one ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He runneth against God, upon the stiff neck of his shield.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “upon the stiff neck of his shield”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Presuming too much upon his own protection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they such who said, “God is our refuge and strength, a Helper in tribulations which have found us out too much”? or in another Psalm, “For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When one learneth that in himself he is nothing, and help in himself has none, arms in him are broken in pieces, wars are made to cease.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such wars then destroyed that Voice of the Most High out of His holy clouds, whereby the earth was moved, and the kingdoms were bowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These wars hath He made to cease unto the end of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall break the bow, and dash in pieces the arms, and burn the shield with fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bow, arms, shield, fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bow is plots; arms, public warfare; shields, vain presuming of self-protection: the fire wherewith they are burned, is that whereof the Lord said, “I am come to send fire on the earth;” of which fire saith the Psalm, “There is nothing hid from the heat thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This fire burning, no arms of ungodliness shall remain in us, needs must all be broken, dashed in pieces, burned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remain thou unharmed, not having any help of thine own; and the more weak thou art, having no arms thine own, the more He taketh thee up, of whom it is said, “The God of Jacob is our taker up.”…But when God taketh us up, doth He send us away unarmed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He armeth us, but with other arms, arms Evangelical, arms of truth, continence, salvation, faith, hope, charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These arms shall we have, but not of ourselves: but the arms which of ourselves we had, are burnt up: yet if by that fire of the Holy Spirit we are kindled, whereof it is said, “He shall burn the shields with fire;” thee, who didst wish to be powerful in thyself, hath God made weak, that He may make thee strong in Him, because in thyself thou wast made weak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And see that I am God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, Not ye, but I am God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I created, I create anew; I formed, I form anew; I made, I make anew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou couldest not make thyself, how canst thou make thyself anew?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This seeth not the contentious tumult of man’s soul; to which contentious tumult is it said, “Be still.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, restrain your souls from contradiction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not argue, and, as it were, arm against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Else yet live thy arms, not yet burned up with fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if they are burned, “Be still;” because ye have not wherewith to fight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if ye be still in yourselves, and from Me seek all, who before presumed on yourselves, then shall ye “see that I am God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just before I said, by the name of earth is signified the nation of the Jews, by the name of sea the other nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The mountains were carried into the heart of the sea; the nations are troubled, the kingdoms are bowed; the Most High gave His Voice, and the earth was moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord of Hosts is with us, the God of Jacob is our taker up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Miracles are done among the heathen, full filled is the faith of the heathen; burned are the arms of human presumption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Still are they, in tranquillity of heart, to acknowledge God the Author of all their gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And after this glorifying, doth He yet desert the people of the Jews? of which saith the Apostle, “I say unto you, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits; that blindness in part is happened unto Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, until the mountains be carried hither, the clouds rain here, the Lord here bows the kingdoms with His thunder, “until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what thereafter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And so all Israel shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, here too observing the same order, “I will be exalted” (saith He) “among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth;” that is, both in the sea, and in the earth, that now might all say what followeth: “the God of Jacob is our taker up.”
